benchmark_honesty_caveat: Autonomous second-round repair-context selector output. Hidden aligned target Lean declarations, statements, names, and proofs are not included. Mathlib signatures here are included only after Lean #check hydration.

checked_signatures.name: Multiset.sort_sorted
checked_signatures.signature:
Multiset.sort_sorted.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} (s : Multiset α) (r : α → α → Prop) [DecidableRel r] [IsTrans α r]
  [Std.Antisymm r] [Std.Total r] : List.Pairwise r (s.sort r)
checked_signatures.why_needed: To prove that the sorted list of parts is weakly decreasing, which is needed for the antitone condition of the padded NPartition.

checked_signatures.name: Multiset.length_sort
checked_signatures.signature:
Multiset.length_sort.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} {s : Multiset α} (r : α → α → Prop) [DecidableRel r] [IsTrans α r]
  [Std.Antisymm r] [Std.Total r] : (s.sort r).length = s.card
checked_signatures.why_needed: To relate the length of the sorted list to the cardinality of the multiset, used in the padding construction.

checked_signatures.name: Multiset.sort_eq
checked_signatures.signature:
Multiset.sort_eq.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} (s : Multiset α) (r : α → α → Prop) [DecidableRel r] [IsTrans α r]
  [Std.Antisymm r] [Std.Total r] : ↑(s.sort r) = s
checked_signatures.why_needed: To show that the multiset coerced from the sorted list equals the original multiset, used to prove sum preservation.

checked_signatures.name: Multiset.sum_coe
checked_signatures.signature: Multiset.sum_coe.{u_3} {M : Type u_3} [AddCommMonoid M] (l : List M) : (↑l).sum = l.sum
checked_signatures.why_needed: To move between list sum and multiset sum, used in sum preservation proofs.

checked_signatures.name: List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_le
checked_signatures.signature:
List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_le.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} {R : α → α → Prop} [Std.Refl R] {l : List α} (h : List.Pairwise R l)
  {a b : Fin l.length} (hab : a ≤ b) : R (l.get a) (l.get b)
checked_signatures.why_needed: To prove the antitone condition for in-range indices of the sorted list.

checked_signatures.name: List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_lt
checked_signatures.signature:
List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_lt.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} {R : α → α → Prop} {l : List α} (h : List.Pairwise R l)
  {a b : Fin l.length} (hab : a < b) : R (l.get a) (l.get b)
checked_signatures.why_needed: Alternative to rel_get_of_le for strict inequality, may be needed for antitone proof.

checked_signatures.name: List.sortedGE_iff_antitone_get
checked_signatures.signature: List.sortedGE_iff_antitone_get.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} {l : List α} [Preorder α] : l.SortedGE ↔ Antitone l.get
checked_signatures.why_needed: To convert between sorted list and antitone get function, used in antitone proof.

checked_signatures.name: Multiset.card_map
checked_signatures.signature: Multiset.card_map.{v, u_1} {α : Type u_1} {β : Type v} (f : α → β) (s : Multiset α) : (Multiset.map f s).card = s.card
checked_signatures.why_needed: To relate the cardinality of the mapped multiset to the original, used in length bound proof.

checked_signatures.name: Finset.card_univ
checked_signatures.signature: Finset.card_univ.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} [Fintype α] : #univ = Fintype.card α
checked_signatures.why_needed: To reason about the cardinality of Fin N, used in length bound proof.

checked_signatures.name: Fintype.card_fin
checked_signatures.why_needed: To compute the cardinality of Fin N, used in length bound proof.

checked_signatures.declaration_line: lemma card_filter_le_iff (s : Multiset α) (P : α → Prop) [DecidablePred P] (n : ℕ) :
checked_signatures.name: Multiset.card_filter_le_iff
checked_signatures.signature:
Multiset.card_filter_le_iff.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} (s : Multiset α) (P : α → Prop) [DecidablePred P] (n : ℕ) :
  (Multiset.filter P s).card ≤ n ↔ ∀ s' ≤ s, n < s'.card → ∃ a ∈ s', ¬P a
checked_signatures.why_needed: Checked fallback candidate for failed request Multiset.card_filter_le: To bound the cardinality of the filtered multiset, used in length bound proof.

checked_signatures.declaration_line: theorem map_count_True_eq_filter_card (s : Multiset α) (p : α → Prop) [DecidablePred p] :
checked_signatures.name: Multiset.map_count_True_eq_filter_card
checked_signatures.signature:
Multiset.map_count_True_eq_filter_card.{u_1} {α : Type u_1} (s : Multiset α) (p : α → Prop) [DecidablePred p] :
  Multiset.count True (Multiset.map p s) = (Multiset.filter p s).card

checked_signatures.name: List.ofFn_get
checked_signatures.signature: List.ofFn_get.{u} {α : Type u} (l : List α) : List.ofFn l.get = l
checked_signatures.why_needed: To relate List.ofFn to get, used in constructing the padded NPartition from a list.

checked_signatures.name: List.sum_ofFn
checked_signatures.signature: List.sum_ofFn.{u_2} {M : Type u_2} [AddCommMonoid M] {n : ℕ} {f : Fin n → M} : (List.ofFn f).sum = ∑ i, f i
checked_signatures.why_needed: To compute the sum of the padded NPartition parts, used in size preservation proof.

checked_signatures.name: Finset.sum_union
checked_signatures.signature:
Finset.sum_union.{u_1, u_4} {ι : Type u_1} {M : Type u_4} {s₁ s₂ : Finset ι} [AddCommMonoid M] {f : ι → M}
  [DecidableEq ι] (h : Disjoint s₁ s₂) : ∑ x ∈ s₁ ∪ s₂, f x = ∑ x ∈ s₁, f x + ∑ x ∈ s₂, f x
checked_signatures.why_needed: To split the sum over Fin N into in-range and out-of-range parts, used in size preservation proof.

checked_signatures.name: Finset.sum_eq_zero
checked_signatures.signature:
Finset.sum_eq_zero.{u_1, u_4} {ι : Type u_1} {M : Type u_4} {s : Finset ι} [AddCommMonoid M] {f : ι → M}
  (h : ∀ x ∈ s, f x = 0) : ∑ x ∈ s, f x = 0
checked_signatures.why_needed: To show that the sum over out-of-range indices (where parts are zero) is zero, used in size preservation proof.

checked_signatures.name: Finset.sum_map
checked_signatures.signature:
Finset.sum_map.{u_1, u_2, u_3} {ι : Type u_1} {κ : Type u_2} {M : Type u_3} [AddCommMonoid M] (s : Finset ι) (e : ι ↪ κ)
  (f : κ → M) : ∑ x ∈ Finset.map e s, f x = ∑ x ∈ s, f (e x)
checked_signatures.why_needed: To relate sums over mapped Finsets, used in size preservation proof.

fallback_resolved_context_requests.expected_signature_or_shape: Multiset.card_filter_le (p : α → Prop) [DecidablePred p] (s : Multiset α) : (s.filter p).card ≤ s.card
fallback_resolved_context_requests.policy: The requested direct identifier did not check, but the listed fallback candidates did. Treat the requested identifier as unavailable and use the checked fallback signatures instead.
fallback_resolved_context_requests.why_needed: To bound the cardinality of the filtered multiset, used in length bound proof.

missing_or_uncertain_context.item: The source requires a notion of 'length' for a `Nat.Partition`. Since `Nat.Partition.length` is not available in Mathlib, we will use `Multiset.card p.parts` as the length. This is already visible in the prior attempt. The main missing piece is a clean definition of the inverse function `fromPartition` that takes a `Nat.Partition n` with `Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N` and produces an `NPartition N` by padding with zeros, and a proof that this is inverse to `toPartition` (after adjusting the index via `size`). The prior attempt's `fromPartition` used `Multiset.sort` which is acceptable, but the antitone proof was incomplete. The requested Mathlib lemmas should enable a complete proof.

The bijection statement should be an `Equiv` between the set of `Nat.Partition n` with length ≤ N and `NPartition N` (with the same sum `n`). The prior attempt used `Function.Bijective` on a sigma type, which is not the intended set. The correct statement is an `Equiv` between `{p : Nat.Partition n // Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N}` and `NPartition N` (with the condition that `size` equals `n`). This requires a proof that `size` of the padded NPartition equals `n`.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: The prior attempt could not prove the antitone condition for the padded NPartition because the proof required reasoning about indices beyond the sorted list length, and the necessary lemmas about zero-padding and monotonicity on out-of-range indices were not available. Additionally, the bijection statement was not correctly formulated as an Equiv between the appropriate subtypes. The visible context provides NPartition structure, Nat.Partition structure, and local helpers (toPartition, filter_ne_zero_sum), but lacks the specific lemmas needed to construct the inverse function and prove the antitone condition using only the checked signatures.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: Define `fromPartition` using `Multiset.sort` to get a decreasing list, then pad with zeros. Prove antitone using `Multiset.sort_sorted` and `List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_le` for in-range indices, and case-split for out-of-range indices. Prove `size` equality using `Multiset.sort_eq` and `Multiset.sum_coe` to relate sorted list sum to multiset sum, and `Nat.Partition.parts_sum`. For the forward direction `toPartition`, use the existing `NPartition.toPartition` and prove the length bound using `Multiset.card_map` and `Finset.card_univ`. Then prove the two inverse lemmas using extensionality and the properties of sorting. Finally, combine into an `Equiv`.

units.failure_analysis: The prior attempt could not prove the antitone condition for the padded NPartition because the proof required reasoning about indices beyond the sorted list length, and the necessary lemmas about zero-padding and monotonicity on out-of-range indices were not available. Additionally, the bijection statement was not correctly formulated as an Equiv between the appropriate subtypes. The visible context provides NPartition structure, Nat.Partition structure, and local helpers (toPartition, filter_ne_zero_sum), but lacks the specific lemmas needed to construct the inverse function and prove the antitone condition using only the checked signatures.

units.repair_strategy_note: Define `fromPartition` using `Multiset.sort` to get a decreasing list, then pad with zeros. Prove antitone using `Multiset.sort_sorted` and `List.Pairwise.rel_get_of_le` for in-range indices, and case-split for out-of-range indices. Prove `size` equality using `Multiset.sort_eq` and `Multiset.sum_coe` to relate sorted list sum to multiset sum, and `Nat.Partition.parts_sum`. For the forward direction `toPartition`, use the existing `NPartition.toPartition` and prove the length bound using `Multiset.card_map` and `Finset.card_univ`. Then prove the two inverse lemmas using extensionality and the properties of sorting. Finally, combine into an `Equiv`.

same_unit_helper_plan.statement_sketch: Given `p : Nat.Partition n` and `h : Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N`, produce an `NPartition N` by padding the sorted parts with zeros.
For `p : Nat.Partition n` and `h : Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N`, `(fromPartition p h).size = n`.
same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: To match the index of the Nat.Partition in the bijection.
To show that the forward direction lands in the set of partitions of length ≤ N.

same_unit_helper_plan.statement_sketch: For `μ : NPartition N`, `fromPartition (NPartition.toPartition μ) (toPartition_length μ) = μ`.
same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: One direction of the bijection inverse.

same_unit_helper_plan.statement_sketch: For `p : Nat.Partition n` and `h : Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N`, `NPartition.toPartition (fromPartition p h) = p` (after transporting the index via `size_fromPartition`).
same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: The other direction of the bijection inverse.

same_unit_helper_plan.statement_sketch: An `Equiv` between `{p : Nat.Partition n // Multiset.card p.parts ≤ N}` and `{μ : NPartition N // μ.size = n}`.
same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: Main claim of the source proposition.

selected_visible_context.why_needed: Defines the bijection: padding a partition of length ℓ ≤ N with N-ℓ zeros, and truncating trailing zeros from an N-partition.
Target type of the bijection; provides `parts : Fin N → ℕ` and `antitone` condition.
Sum of parts of an N-partition; used to match the index of `Nat.Partition`.
Existing conversion from `NPartition N` to `Nat.Partition μ.size`; this is the forward direction (truncating zeros).
Extensionality for NPartition; needed to prove equality of NPartitions.
Used in `toPartition` to show sum is preserved; may be reused.

same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: One direction of the bijection inverse.

same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: The other direction of the bijection inverse.

same_unit_helper_plan.why_needed: Main claim of the source proposition.
